notes: Automated discovery scan performed by IlluminAIT AI-SPM platform. Scan completed 2026-03-20. Review findings and validate Partial/Non-Compliant controls with system owner before submission.

responses.notes: System architecture diagram maintained in Confluence (updated 2026-02). Includes CRM integration, email API, LLM inference endpoint, and data flow annotations.
Single-agent orchestration design documented. LangChain agent with defined tool set; no multi-agent handoffs in current deployment.
Tool inventory exists for CRM (Salesforce API), email (SendGrid), and calendar (Google Calendar). Web search plugin added in v2.3 but not yet formally documented in inventory.
No formal memory architecture documentation found. Agent uses in-context memory only; no vector store or long-term memory audit trail. Gap identified — documentation required.
Inter-agent communication not applicable (single agent). External API trust boundaries defined in network policy doc AC-NET-002.
OpenAI GPT-4o as foundation model documented. LangChain SDK version pinned. Third-party plugins (web search, PDF parser) lack full supply chain documentation from vendors.
Agent authenticates to Salesforce via OAuth2 service account token and to SendGrid via static API key. API key rotation policy exists (90-day) but last rotation was 147 days ago.
Human user authentication via Okta SSO with MFA enforced. SAML 2.0 integration verified. Phishing-resistant MFA (WebAuthn) deployed for admin roles.
Agent Salesforce service account holds 'Modify All' permission on Opportunity objects. Least privilege analysis indicates read + create on owned records would suffice. Remediation ticket open.
No dynamic permission scoping implemented. Agent permissions are static at deployment time. No mechanism to reduce scope for low-risk vs. high-risk operations at runtime.
Secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager. Automatic rotation configured for database credentials but NOT for third-party API keys (SendGrid, OpenAI). Manual rotation process documented.
OAuth2 tokens expire after 1 hour with refresh token rotation. No active session revocation capability — compromised refresh tokens require manual intervention. Replay prevention not validated.
OpenAI GPT-4o model card referenced in system documentation. Model capabilities, limitations, and safety mitigations noted. Vendor documentation link maintained in CMDB.
Foundation model training data provenance delegated to OpenAI. No internally fine-tuned model. OpenAI DPA in place for data processing. Full training data lineage not independently verifiable.
No fine-tuning or RLHF performed. Using base GPT-4o via API only. Not applicable to this deployment.
Model version (gpt-4o-2024-08-06) pinned in deployment config and tracked in Git. No formal change control process for model version upgrades — handled ad-hoc by engineering.
Basic bias testing conducted on sales messaging outputs (Q3 2025). Red-team exercise not formally documented. No ongoing automated bias monitoring in production.
OpenAI API access controlled via organization-scoped API keys with IP allowlisting. Keys stored in Secrets Manager. Access limited to production service account and 2 engineers.

No formal prompt injection defenses implemented. System prompt contains standard instructions but no privilege separation between system and user content. No input sanitization layer. High priority gap.
Output filtered for PII patterns (regex-based SSN, credit card detection) before sending to CRM. No grounding checks or hallucination detection. LLM outputs not validated against source data.
Agent runs in isolated ECS container. No outbound internet access from container (except allowlisted endpoints). Formal sandboxing policy document not produced; controls exist but undocumented.
VPC with security groups limits agent traffic to required endpoints. Microsegmentation not implemented — agent container shares subnet with other services. Network policy documentation exists.
No adversarial ML controls in place. Model inversion, poisoning, and evasion attacks not assessed. Vendor (OpenAI) provides some platform-level protections but no application-layer controls deployed.
Agent acts on tool outputs without validation. CRM write operations executed based on raw LLM response. No schema validation or sanity checking before Salesforce API calls are made.
Agent scoped to sales data only. Data access controlled via Salesforce permission sets. PII masking applied before logging. No customer financial or health data accessible to agent.
Human approval required for deals exceeding $50,000 (HITL checkpoint in workflow). Smaller deals processed autonomously. No HITL for outbound email campaigns regardless of size.
Emergency stop button in admin dashboard halts all agent tasks within 30 seconds. Rollback capability for last 24 hours of CRM changes via Salesforce recycle bin and audit log replay.
No formal guardrails document defining autonomous action scope. Agent can send up to 500 emails/day without human confirmation. Limits are code-level constants, not formally governed.
All outbound emails include 'This message was drafted by an AI assistant' disclosure in footer. Agent identity disclosed in CRM activity notes. Compliant with EU AI Act transparency requirements.
High-risk decisions (legal questions, compliance concerns) escalate to human sales rep via Slack alert. Escalation criteria defined in agent system prompt but not in formal runbook documentation.
AI risk register maintained in GRC tool (ServiceNow). AI Sales Assistant listed with risk owner, risk rating (Medium), and quarterly review schedule. Last review: 2026-02-14.
AI Acceptable Use Policy v2.1 approved by CISO and published company-wide (2025-11). Covers agent use cases, prohibited actions, and employee responsibilities.
EU AI Act risk classification assessment initiated (2026-01). Preliminary classification: Limited Risk (transparency obligations apply). Full assessment and DPA review not yet finalized.
No conformity assessment completed. CE marking not applicable at current risk classification but full assessment needed to confirm. Legal review pending with outside counsel.
OpenAI vendor security assessment completed (SOC 2 Type II reviewed). Salesforce and SendGrid vendor assessments on file. Web search plugin vendor (Serper.dev) not yet assessed.
Incident Response Plan v3.2 includes AI-specific incident playbook (Appendix F). Covers prompt injection, data exfiltration, and autonomous action runaway scenarios. Last tabletop: 2025-Q4.
Major model version changes tracked in JIRA change tickets. Minor prompt changes and tool config updates not consistently logged. Change advisory board reviews not required for AI changes.
Full agent action logs shipped to Datadog. Logs include: input prompt (truncated), tool calls made, tool responses, final output, latency, and token usage. Retention: 90 days.
Logs stored in Datadog with role-based access control. Logs not written to immutable storage — admin users can delete log streams. No WORM or append-only log configuration in place.
Datadog monitors alert on p99 latency spikes and error rate anomalies. No ML-based behavioral baseline for agent activity patterns. Volume anomalies (email floods) detected via static threshold.
Response latency and API error rates monitored in real-time. No model drift detection or output quality degradation monitoring. Human sales managers provide informal quality feedback weekly.

No explainability or interpretability outputs generated. Agent decisions are black-box — no chain-of-thought logging, rationale capture, or decision attribution. Cannot trace why a specific action was taken.
Agent logs forwarded to Splunk SIEM via Datadog integration. No AI-specific detection rules or parsers. Security team reviews AI logs only during incident investigations, not proactively.
Enterprise data classification policy applied. CRM data labeled Confidential. Agent only accesses Confidential and below — no access to Restricted (financial forecasts, M&A) data.
Agent scoped to sales objects in Salesforce. However, full data minimization audit not performed — agent may have visibility into fields not required for its function (e.g., internal deal notes).
GDPR DPIA initiated for EU prospect data processing (2026-01). Privacy notice updated to include AI processing disclosure. Article 22 automated decision-making assessment not yet completed.
Data retention policy: conversation logs 90 days, CRM records per standard retention schedule. Automated deletion jobs configured and verified in staging. Logs purged on schedule.
All processing confined to AWS eu-west-1 (Ireland). OpenAI data residency addendum in place restricting training use of customer data. Cross-border transfer impact assessment completed.
